source.description: Planned six-panel generated raster marker process sheet for the July 11, 2026 current lesson.
source.notes: One upright surfboard stays centered at a slight diagonal on warm paper, with bold handmade marker texture and no face.

process_strategy: The process sets the long board silhouette and fin first, then establishes the center stripe, leash loop, black outline, small wave marks, and cyan/coral fill before the final panel only strengthens existing marks.

final_elements.name: long surfboard silhouette
final_elements.description: A tall rounded board with a pointed nose and rounded tail.

final_elements.name: small bottom fin
final_elements.description: A simple triangular fin below the board tail.

final_elements.name: center stripe panel
final_elements.description: A wide curved stripe running down the established board.

final_elements.name: leash loop
final_elements.description: A short curled leash loop attached near the board tail.

final_elements.name: thick black board outline
final_elements.description: A confident marker contour around existing board, fin, stripe, and leash shapes.

final_elements.name: simple wave marks
final_elements.description: A few small blue wave curves below the board.

final_elements.name: cyan and coral marker fill
final_elements.description: Visible cyan board fill, a coral stripe, and blue wave accents.

frames.visible_job: Places the tall surfboard silhouette and small bottom fin with a light construction pass.
Adds a single curved center stripe inside the established board outline.
Adds the tail leash loop, then traces only existing board and stripe shapes with a thick black marker.
Adds a few simple wave curves under the already inked board.
Fills the existing board cyan, stripe coral, and waves blue with visible marker streaks.

final_step.visible_job: Only reinforces the existing black edge, leash loop, stripe, fin, wave marks, and already established cyan/coral/blue fills.

rejection_checks: No fin, stripe, leash, wave mark, color area, or outline may first appear in the final panel.
The surfboard must keep the same upright angle, crop, and proportions in every frame.
The finish must remain handmade marker art, not a clean vector icon or product poster.
